title: The Resilience Quality Index (RQI) as a Tool to Identify Risk Factors at a Military Installation
authors_full: Amanda Straus, DBH, Rachel Cassalia, PhD, & Alondra Cruz, MA
authors_short: Amanda Straus DBH Rachel Cassalia & Alondra Cruz

summary.hook: This poster presents the Resilience Quality Index (RQI), described as a real-time assessment tool for military installations to monitor risk and protective factors related to...
summary.summary_30s: This poster presents the Resilience Quality Index (RQI), described as a real-time assessment tool for military installations to monitor risk and protective factors related to service members’ health, resilience, and readiness. A customized six-question RQI was piloted at a military installation and distributed online via Qualtrics on two occasions (two iterations; total n=124).

key_numbers: This poster presents the Resilience Quality Index (RQI), described as a real-time assessment tool for military...
takeaways: Total respondents: n=124 across two iterations (58 in Iteration 1; 66 in Iteration 2).
Overall risk categorization (Table 1): Very Low 15 (12%), Low 48 (39%), Some 43 (35%), Moderate 15 (12%), High 3 (2%).
The RQI can inform leadership decisions by providing timely, structured insight into service member concerns.
